options.name: --password
options.description: Password or other sensitive data to be redacted from binlog. Multiple options are supported. Has to be specified if common patterns opted out with --no-common-patterns.

options.name: --input
options.description: Input binary log file name. Or a directory to inspect for all existing binlogs. If not specified current directory is assumed.

options.name: --output
options.description: Output binary log file name. If not specified, replaces the input file in place - overwrite option needs to be specified in such case.

options.name: --overwrite
options.description: Replace the output file if it already exists. Replace the input file if the output file is not specified.

options.name: --dryrun
options.description: Performs the operation in-memory and outputs what would be performed.

options.name: --recurse
options.description: Recurse given path (or current dir if none) for all binlogs. Applies only when single input file is not specified.

options.name: --logsecrets
options.description: Logs what secrets have been detected and replaced. This should be used only for test/troubleshooting purposes!

options.name: --skip-embedded-files
options.description: Binlog embedded files will not be processed by the redactor.

options.name: --identify-replacements
options.description: If set - the redacted values will be distinguishable (e.g. 'REDACTED__Gcp-Token', 'REDACTED__USERNAME') instead all same ('*******')

options.name: --no-common-patterns
options.description: If set - redactor will not attempt to autodetect possible sensitive data based (username, tokens/secrets with known formats, etc.). Specific passwords to redact must be specified in such case (with -p)

options.name: --verbosity
options.description: Sets the verbosity level. Allowed values are q[uiet], m[inimal], n[ormal], and diag[nostic]. [default: normal]
